name: Pi

form_factor.comment: Terminal CLI via npm or curl installer and SDK docs for embedding. Per-form-factor links: CLI: https://pi.dev/docs/latest/quickstart and README install section; SDK: https://pi.dev/docs/latest/sdk.

released_in.comment: Pi changelog shows first listed public release as 0.74.0 dated 2026-05-07.

latest_major_update.comment: Latest release v0.80.3 (June 30, 2026) adds Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 support through inherited Anthropic-compatible and Bedrock provider catalogs with adaptive thinking enabled.

rules.comment: Loads `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` from `~/.pi/agent/`, ancestors, and cwd. Supports global and project context files with path-based precedence.

skills.comment: Discovers skills from `~/.pi/agent/skills/`, `~/.agents/skills/`, `.pi/skills/`, and `.agents/skills/`, including `SKILL.md` directories. Supports `/skill:name` command registration and cross-harness imports (e.g. Claude/ Codex).

hooks.comment: Extension API supports lifecycle/tool hooks via TypeScript extensions, including tool event hooks and custom event handlers. Full extension-based hook model from docs and examples.

mcp_servers.comment: README explicitly states no MCP. MCP-like capabilities can be built via extensions instead.

custom_commands.comment: Rich slash-command UI includes built-ins like `/model`, `/resume`, `/tree`, `/fork`, `/clone`, `/compact`, `/export`, `/share`, `/settings`, and `/login`.

goal_command.comment: No `/goal` concept is documented; README is explicit about what is *not* built in (including plan mode and no-permission prompts).

subagents.comment: README says "No sub-agents." suggests using spawned pi instances/context tools instead of native subagent orchestration.

monitor.comment: Pi docs explicitly say there are no native subagents, and reviewed docs do not show a Monitor-style background watch primitive.

model_selection.comment: Model/provider selection is fully exposed with `/model` in interactive mode and `--provider`, `--model`, `--models` CLI options.

approval_mode.comment: Philosophy page says there are no permission popups by default and no approval mode is documented as a first-class control.

sandbox_mode.comment: No sandbox mode setting is documented; README points users to tmux/container-like patterns instead of built-in sandbox controls.

resume.comment: Sessions can be resumed with `pi -r/--resume`, `--session`, and interactive `/resume` + `/fork`/`/clone` branch flow.

continue.comment: Continue latest session with `-c/--continue`; also supports session browsing and tree-based continuation commands in-session.

non_interactive.comment: Supports print mode (`-p/--print`) and machine-friendly transports (`--mode json`, `--mode rpc`) for scripted/CI usage.

output_format.comment: `--mode json` outputs JSON lines and JSON mode docs exist for event streaming consumers and integrations.

statusline.comment: Interactive UI includes a built-in footer/status area but no documented customization API for a user-defined status line.

telemetry.comment: Telemetry is configurable via `enableInstallTelemetry` and can be disabled; `PI_SKIP_VERSION_CHECK` and offline modes control network checks.

custom_model_provider.comment: Supports custom provider/API integration through `~/.pi/agent/models.json` and custom provider registration hooks for OpenAI-compatible, Anthropic, Google, and custom flows.

notes: Pi is a minimal terminal coding harness with extensibility-first defaults (extensions, skills, prompt templates, themes, packages), plus SDK and RPC/event-stream integrations.

pricing.value: Free MIT/local tool; BYOK/provider or local-model costs
pricing.comment: Pi's official page documents provider authentication via API keys or OAuth. Current official page and repo/package search did not show a first-party paid model subscription; use BYOK/provider/local inference.

no_account_required.comment: Pi supports custom providers/models including Ollama, vLLM, LM Studio, and proxies, so local usage need not require a Pi-hosted account.

can_use_other_subscriptions.comment: Pi documents subscription-based providers via OAuth plus API-key providers and auth-file/env configuration.

hosted_agent.comment: CLI is local-first; no dedicated hosted runtime platform is documented.

transcripts.comment: Sessions are saved automatically to `~/.pi/agent/sessions/` with session IDs and `/session` command showing file and metadata.

keyboard_shortcuts.comment: Research needed. Check official documentation for keybinding customization options.

theme_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for theme/appearance customization options.

auto_save.comment: Research needed. Check for auto-save or session persistence features.

debugging_output.comment: Research needed. Check for debug mode or verbose logging options.

context_window.comment: Research needed. Check for configurable context length or token limits.

code_formatting.comment: Research needed. Check for formatter/linter integration or auto-format options.

git_integration.comment: Research needed. Check for git command support or version control integration.

performance_mode.comment: Research needed. Check for caching, optimization, or resource management settings.

ui_customization.comment: Research needed. Check for UI layout, sidebar, or panel customization.
